compiled_state.title: Write wrapper-skill parity enforcement test
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Create tests/scripts/test-wrapper-parity.sh that verifies every skill with user-invocable: true in its SKILL.md has a corresponding .claude/commands/<name>.md wrapper file. This is a structural guard test that catches future drift when new skills are added without wrappers.

### Test approach
1. Grep for user-invocable: true in all plugins/dso/skills/*/SKILL.md files
2. Extract skill names from directory paths
3. For each skill name, check .claude/commands/<name>.md exists
4. Report any missing wrappers and exit non-zero if any found

Also test the reverse: any .claude/commands/<name>.md should correspond to a skill directory in plugins/dso/skills/<name>/.

### TDD Exemption
This task IS writing a test — no separate RED test needed for a test-writing task. The test itself is a metadata/schema validation structural guard (acceptable per behavioral test exceptions).

### File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-wrapper-parity.sh (create)
- .test-index (modify: add entry)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] test-wrapper-parity.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-wrapper-parity.sh
- [ ] Parity test passes (all skills have wrappers)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-wrapper-parity.sh
- [ ] Reverse parity also checked (no orphan wrappers)
  Verify: grep -q 'orphan\|reverse\|no matching skill' tests/scripts/test-wrapper-parity.sh
- [ ] .test-index entry exists for this test
  Verify: grep -q 'test-wrapper-parity.sh' .test-index

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written (this IS a test) ✓
